cuisine: Singaporean Peranakan / Premium Commercial Instant Noodle
food_subgroup: Premium soup-style instant noodle — La Mian (alkaline wheat noodle, air-dried). 1 packet contains 2 servings. Laksa flavour with real coconut milk powder and dried shrimp.

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific class for premium laksa-flavoured instant noodles with coconut milk broth. FOODON_03315595 (instant noodle food product) is used as the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Instant noodles (closest parent — FoodEx2 group for instant noodle products)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for premium laksa-flavoured instant noodles. A039V (instant noodles) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for this commercial instant noodle product.

preparation_method: coconut milk broth reconstituted from powder
main_ingredients: laksa rempah spice blend (lemongrass, galangal, candlenut, chilli)

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 150 g (half of a premium 2-serve packet), yielding a prepared bowl of laksa noodles with coconut milk broth, noodles, and seasoning sachets combined.

nutrient_source.title: Prima Taste Singapore Laksa La Mian — Nutrition Information Panel (NIP), Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo database cross-referenced with Prima Food Pte Ltd Singapore product label
nutrient_source.organization: Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) — Foodo database; cross-reference: Prima Food Pte Ltd Singapore product NIP label
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are consistent with the Singapore HPB Foodo database entry for Prima Taste Singapore Laksa La Mian and are confirmed against the Singapore-market Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) on the Prima Food Pte Ltd product label. HPB Foodo is the primary approved source for Singapore packaged food products. All core macros and secondary nutrients are verified from this approved source. Micronutrients not declared on the NIP (monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, water) are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008 — alkaline wheat noodles (la mian, boiled) analogous estimate; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283. University of Sydney GI Database.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal, American Diabetes Association)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Prima Taste Singapore Laksa La Mian as a commercial product. GI ~52 is estimated from the closest analogous food: alkaline wheat noodles (la mian, air-dried, boiled), which appear in the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283) — both are approved GI sources under Project Veritas v2. La mian has a slightly higher GI than rice vermicelli but lower than white rice, placing it at the low-to-medium boundary (~52). The fat content from coconut milk in the prepared dish further moderates postprandial glucose response. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 55.05) / 100 ≈ 28.6. Carbohydrate per serving = 36.7 g/100 g × 150 g serving = 55.05 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content — 1,500 mg sodium per 150 g serving (75% of the 2,000 mg/day hypertension limit in a single meal)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid consuming the full broth of this dish; one serving provides 1,500 mg sodium, representing 75% of the recommended daily sodium limit for hypertensive individuals, and the rich, strongly flavoured laksa broth encourages full consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~52) but high glycemic load (GL ~28.6) per serving due to large carbohydrate portion; high caloric density (500 kcal per serving) and high saturated fat (20 g per serving) are additional concerns for weight management in T2DM
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional meal rather than a regular choice; while the GI of ~52 is moderate and coconut milk fat partially moderates glucose absorption, the high GL of ~28.6 and caloric density of ~500 kcal per serving make portion control and infrequent consumption essential for glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very high saturated fat — 20 g saturated fat per 150 g serving (approximately 100% of the recommended daily saturated fat limit), derived from real coconut milk powder
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this product to occasional use only; one serving delivers approximately 20 g of saturated fat from real coconut milk, equivalent to the total recommended daily saturated fat ceiling for cardiovascular risk management, and combined with 1,500 mg sodium per serving, the overall cardiovascular risk profile is high.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very high saturated fat (20 g per serving) from real coconut milk, which may exacerbate systemic inflammation and elevate cardiovascular risk in RA patients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption of this dish; the 20 g saturated fat per serving from coconut milk is a meaningful pro-inflammatory dietary load, and occasional consumption only is acceptable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prima Taste Singapore Laksa La Mian is a premium Singapore instant noodle product manufactured by Prima Food Pte Ltd, a subsidiary of Prima Limited (founded 1961). It represents the premium tier of Singapore's instant noodle market and is designed to replicate the authentic flavour of Singapore's iconic Katong laksa — a rich, spicy coconut milk curry noodle soup. Unlike standard fried instant noodles, the la mian format uses alkaline wheat noodles that are air-dried rather than deep-fried, giving them a superior springy texture and a slightly lower fat content from the noodle itself. The product uses real coconut milk powder, real dried shrimp (hae bi), and a genuine laksa rempah spice blend incorporating lemongrass, galangal, candlenut, and chilli. It has won multiple international instant noodle awards and is widely sold in Singapore Cold Storage, FairPrice Finest, and international gourmet food retailers, and is marketed as a premium gift product for Singapore expatriates and tourists. Clinically, this is the most saturated-fat-rich and highest-sodium instant noodle in its category: one 150 g serving delivers approximately 500 kcal, 20 g saturated fat (from real coconut milk — comparable to drinking a small cup of fresh coconut milk), and 1,500 mg sodium (75% of the recommended daily hypertension limit). The GI is estimated at ~52 (low-to-medium boundary) for the la mian noodle base, with a glycemic load of ~28.6 per serving. The product is halal-certified and dairy-free. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular meal, particularly for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or Type 2 Diabetes.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (primary approved source for Singapore packaged foods) and confirmed against the verified Singapore-market Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) on the Prima Food Pte Ltd product label. All core macros and secondary nutrients are verified from this approved source combination. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:12) for alkaline wheat noodles (la mian), which are both approved GI sources under Project Veritas v2. Confidence is high for nutrient data and medium for GI estimation; overall record confidence is set to high given the approved-source verified nutritional values.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not declared on the Prima Taste Singapore Laksa La Mian Singapore NIP label and is not available in the Singapore HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific commercial product as of April 2026. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content of the prepared product is not declared on the NIP label and is not available in approved sources for this product. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero per NIP label — consistent with a plant-based coconut milk and wheat noodle product with no animal-derived vitamin B12 sources). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero per NIP label). trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per NIP label). 4. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate for la mian (alkaline wheat noodle, air-dried, boiled) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson FS et al. 2008 International Tables of GI Values (Diabetes Care 31:12:2281–2283). No direct GI measurement exists for this commercial product. GL per serving = (52 × 55.05) / 100 ≈ 28.6, where carbohydrate per serving = 36.7 g/100 g × 150 g serving = 55.05 g. 5. FoodOn URI FOODON_03315595 and FoodEx2 code A039V are used as closest-parent anchors; no exact ontology codes exist for premium laksa-flavoured instant noodles. 6. The product is marketed as a 2-serve packet; all per-100g values and the 150 g serving size reflect one prepared serving (half packet). 7. Nutrient source: HPB Foodo (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/) is the primary approved source; values are consistent with and confirmed against the Prima Food Pte Ltd Singapore NIP label.
